name: Molasses Cookies
recipeInstructions:
There are a variety of shapes and fillings you can use when making hand-shaped cookies. These require more time to make than other types because each cookie must be individually shaped. It's worth the extra effort, and you may enjoy shaping them as much as eating them! Dark, fragrant, dunk-in-milk-or-coffee cookies that your great grandmother would love.
1. In a large bowl, cream margarine or shortening with sugar until smooth.
2. Add egg and blend well.
3. Stir in molasses and spices; mix thoroughly.
4. Gradually add flour and baking soda, stirring until combined.
5. Chill the dough for 30 minutes or overnight, covered tightly.
6. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
7. Shape the dough into small balls, roll in extra sugar, and place 2-inches apart on a cookie sheet.
8. Bake for 10-12 minutes, then remove from the oven and cool on wire racks.
Yield: 2 1/2 dozen cookies.
